function decomposeChunk(T) {
    const words = [];

```javascript
function isLetter(char) {
    return char >= 'a' && char <= 'z';
}

function decomposeChunk(T) {
    const words = [];
    let i = 0;
    
    while (i < T.length) {
        // 1. Find the start of a potential word segment (must start with a letter)
        let i_start = i;
        while (i_start < T.length && !isLetter(T[i_start])) {
            i_start++;
        }

        if (i_start === T.length) break; // No more letters found
        
        // 2. Build the word W starting at i_start
        let W = "";
        let j = i_start;
        
        while (j < T.length) {
            const c = T[j];
            
            if (isLetter(c)) {
                W += c;
                j++;
                continue;
            }
            
            if (c === '-') {
                // Check hyphen validity
                // Preceding character must be a letter (since we started at i_start=letter, and we are only adding letters/valid hyphens)
                const prevIsLetter = (j > i_start && isLetter(T[j-1])); 
                // Succeeding character must be a letter
                const nextIsLetter = (j + 1 < T.length && isLetter(T[j+1]));
                
                if (prevIsLetter && nextIsLetter) {
                    W += c;
                    j++;
                    continue;
                } else {
                    // Invalid hyphen, word ends here
                    break;
                }
            }
            
            // Should not happen if T only contains letters and hyphens, 
            // but included for robustness if the chunk extraction was flawed.
            break; 
        }
        
        if (W.length > 0) {
            words.push(W);
        }
        
        // 3. Update i: start the next search from where the current word construction ended
        // If the word was empty (which shouldn't happen if i_start was found), we advance past the current index.
        if (W.length === 0) {
             i++;
        } else {
             i = j;
        }
    }
    return words;
}


function solve(chunks, queries) {
    // Step 1: Concatenate
    const s = chunks.join('');
    
    // Step 2 & 3: Extract all words
    const allWords = [];
    let currentChunk = "";
    
    for (let i = 0; i < s.length; i++) {
        const char = s[i];
        
        if (isLetter(char) || char === '-') {
            currentChunk += char;
        } else {
            // Separator encountered. Process the chunk.
            if (currentChunk.length > 0) {
                const decomposed = decomposeChunk(currentChunk);
                allWords.push(...decomposed);
            }
            currentChunk = "";
        }
    }
    
    // Process the last chunk if it exists
    if (currentChunk.length > 0) {
        const decomposed = decomposeChunk(currentChunk);
        allWords.push(...decomposed);
    }
    
    // Step 4: Count occurrences
    const resultsMap = new Map();
    for (const q of queries) {
        resultsMap.set(q, 0);
    }
    
    // Iterate over all extracted words and count matches for all queries
    for (const W of allWords) {
        for (const q of queries) {
            if (q.length === 0 || W.length < q.length) continue;
            
            let count = 0;
            // Count overlapping occurrences of q in W
            let start = 0;
            while (start <= W.length - q.length) {
                // Optimized substring comparison using indexOf/slice might be faster 
                // than manual loop, but we need to handle overlapping correctly.
                
                // Manual check for overlapping matches:
                let match = true;
                for (let k = 0; k < q.length; k++) {
                    if (W[start + k] !== q[k]) {
                        match = false;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                
                if (match) {
                    count++;
                    // Crucial: Move start by 1 to allow overlapping matches
                    start++; 
                } else {
                    start++;
                }
            }
            
            if (count > 0) {
                resultsMap.set(q, resultsMap.get(q) + count);
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Format output array
    const ans = [];
    for (const q of queries) {
        ans.push(resultsMap.get(q) || 0);
    }
    
    return ans;
}
